title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Cutt summons Rupert Bettman, the private secretary to Sir Osborne. Bettman, a serious man wearing glasses, is nicknamed "the chimpanzee" by his former schoolmate Jimmy Sycamore. After Bettman departs, Jimmy joins a group by the lake consisting of Bill Everly, Ronnie DeVille, and three girls named Helen, Nancy, and "Socks." The group discusses a plan to play a prank on Gerry Wade, who has overslept. While Ronnie suggests creating a loud commotion to wake the entire house, Socks argues for a more subtle approach. Bettman arrives and suggests using an alarm clock to wake Gerry, leading the group to decide to purchase multiple clocks.

Jimmy investigates the restaurant and finds Gerry eating toast and jam. To prevent Gerry from joining the group, Jimmy, Nancy, and Helen attempt to distract a bewildered Mrs. Cutt. Gerry manages to avoid her lectures on punctuality and gardening by engaging her in conversation.

Meanwhile, Bill, Ronnie, and the girls travel to the Basing Mall. They encounter the store owner, Mr. DeGroatroyd, who tries to sell them high-quality timepieces. Ignoring his advice regarding reliability, the group specifically requests loud clocks. Jimmy tests several models, causing the store to echo with deafening alarms. The group eventually selects the six loudest alarm clocks, and Ronnie notes they will also buy one for Bettman.
